title: The Quack Doctor’s Hope
description: On pages 14–17, this part focuses on the quack doctor’s hope, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: The Quack Doctor’s Hope. Hiriluk reaches out to Chopper and gives him a place to belong, while Drum Kingdom’s cruelty remains in the background. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: It's your own damn fault for following me even after I told you not to!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: What kind of doctor kicks his own patient!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: If it weren't for me, you would've already been dead!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Damn Blue- Nosed Poor Excuse for a Rein- deer!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: that was the first time I've got into an argument like this...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh eh... I suppose it would be. An

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Well, there's a first time for everything. Including...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: And your first making- up after fighting.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Hey, you can't turn into your giant form! That's cheating!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: After a full year of treatment,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: I now declare you're in perfect health!

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: You've been fully treated, haven't you!?

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: So I've got no obligations to keep looking after you anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Now go spend the rest of your life however you want.

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Just get out of my lab, I've got work to do.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: I promise I won't ever be a bother so please let me in!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: I promise I'll massage your shoulders everyday, pour you tea, and even do the cleaning! So please! Please let me back in!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: I've got no friends and no place to go! Please, doctor!

summary: This part highlights the quack doctor’s hope and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
